name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Linux File Deletion
notes:
This Building Block detects file deletion using "rm", "shred" or "unlink" commands. Adversaries may delete files using these commands to cover up their activities.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: File Deletion
SIGMA Description: Detects file deletion using "rm", "shred" or "unlink" commands which are used often by adversaries to delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Linux File Deletion on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [rm or shred or unlink]
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Linux File Deletion on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [rm or shred or unlink]
